title: SpecGate Component Spec
description: Schema for SpecGate .spec.yaml files. Specs declare operations (each with their own inputs/outputs) and test cases that exercise them. The harness resolves the binding, compiles annotated code, runs operations, and compares actual traces against expected.

binding.description: Binding(s) for this spec. A string for a single binding, or a list for multiple implementations.

depends_on.description: List of spec names this spec depends on.

types.description: Named type definitions shared across operations.

operations.description: Named operations, each declaring its own inputs, outputs, and outcome.

kind.description: Operation kind. 'setup' creates state objects. 'command' runs a shell command. Default is a regular annotated operation.

async.description: Whether this operation is async. The harness validates this against annotations — mismatch is an error. The generated runner uses an async runtime when true.

inputs.description: Named input declarations for this operation.

outputs.description: List of observable outputs this operation can produce. Each item is either a bare event name (string), a name with scalar type (name: type), or a name with structured type definition.

typeDefinition.description: Inline type definition for structured outputs. Used when an output is a collection or complex type.

type.description: Type kind: string, i32, i64, f64, bool, list, map, set, or a reference to a named type.

fields.description: For list element types or inline struct definitions. Keys are field names, values are types.

items.description: Element type for set types. A string (type name) or inline type definition.

values.description: Value type for map types. A string (type name) or inline type definition.

desc.description: Implementation constraint or design intent in plain language.

verify.description: Steps to manually verify this constraint is met.

level.description: Normative strength. Affects what happens when the annotation is missing: must=error, should=warn, may=skip. If implemented, case must pass regardless of level.

source.description: Provenance metadata linking this case to source specification assertions. Opaque to the harness, consumed by reporting tools.

assertion_ids.description: IDs of source spec assertions this case verifies.

section.description: Section reference in the source specification.

operation.description: Operation to invoke (must match a key in the operations block).

setup.description: Setup operation name (string) or map of named setups with inputs.

inputs.description: Input values for this case (validated against the operation's declared inputs).

expected.description: Expected results. A list of trace assertions using $-prefixed directives ($run, $unordered, $anywhere) and bare event assertions (name: value). Or an object with structured output for function operations.

steps.description: Ordered step sequence. Each item is a sequential operation or a concurrent block of parallel operations.

concurrent.description: Operations that run in parallel. The block completes (barrier) before the next sequential step.

operation.description: Operation name (must match a key in the operations block).

expected.description: Per-step expected trace assertions. Optional — if omitted, the case-level expected covers the whole sequence.
